text: What if I told you EPO altitude might not be what you think? EPO, or erythropoietin, is a hormone that boosts red blood cells. But how does altitude tie into this? At high altitudes, less oxygen triggers EPO production. More red blood cells, right? But it’s more complex than that. Studies show mixed results on performance. Some athletes benefit, others don’t. So the science isn’t black and white. It’s a blend of biology and individual response. Curious about more science mysteries? Hit like and follow for more revelations!
